title: Long awaited outside audit complete of Mayor Walsh's payroll modernization attempt
summary: Syracuse Common Council scheduled a Finance Committee meeting (June 13, 2025) to review and publicly release an external audit of Mayor Walsh's five-year payroll modernization initiative, which has cost taxpayers over $8 million and remains incomplete. Foxpointe Solutions completed the commissioned audit, with the meeting marking a potential turning point as the administration issues new RFPs to complete remaining project components.

political_signal.description: Council exercising fiscal oversight through formal audit and public meeting on troubled $8M+ initiative; escalating oversight pattern indicated by prior AG review and whistleblower disputes

processing_notes: Dollar amount extracted as '$8 million+' from article text; precise total may be clarified in audit release
Meeting date inferred as Thursday June 13, 2025 based on article publication date June 8-9, 2025
Multiple prior I-Team investigations referenced; core issue involves incomplete modernization, cost overruns, consultant dependencies, and administrative changes
New RFP issuance signals project restart/pivot rather than resolution
PROVISIONAL tag 'escalating' applied due to pattern of investigations and administrative fallout indicated by article links
